Name: Bitcoin
Description: Created in January 2009, Bitcoin is the first decentralized cryptocurrency. Bitcoin serves as a completely digital payment system without any central authority taking part in the process. Bitcoin uses cryptography to keep itself secure, and Bitcoin transactions can be transparently viewed on the blockchain. In addition, the total supply limit of Bitcoin will stop at 21 million coins.

Name: Ethereum
Description: Ethereum is a smart contract platform powered by its native currency: ETH, also called Ether or Ethereum. Because Ethereum allows decentralized applications (DApps) to be built on its blockchain, its network empowers from decentralized finance (DeFi) applications, non-fungible tokens (NFTs) to blockchain games.

Name: Build 'N Build
Description: Initially created as an ERC20 token on the Ethereum network, BNB is now a native token gas token that powers the BNB chain. BNB can also be used as transaction fees on the Binance Exchange, to trade with other cryptocurrencies, to book travel arrangements, buying virtual gifts or to be used as collaterals for loans on numerous platforms. They recently rebranded the chain name to "Build 'N Build."

Name: XRP
Description: XRP is a digital asset which bridges two different currencies quickly and efficiently. XRP is the native currency of XRP Ledger, a blockchain-based, open-sourced, distributed protocol that has a transaction speed of 3-5 seconds. The company behind XRP is known as Ripple, who claims to offer “secure, instantly and nearly free global financial transactions of any size with no chargebacks".

Name: Cardano
Description: Cardano is a smart contract platform whose foundation focuses on a science-based approach. Their papers are peer-reviewed by experts internationally to validate their method. Cardano, moreover, uses the Ouroboros POS system which divides time into epochs, thus increasing scalability. In addition, the Cardano network divides itself into subnetworks to handle more transactions. It is currently deploying its first smart contracts to compete with other layer 1 protocols.

Name: Solana
Description: Solana is a blockchain created to host decentralized, scalable applications. The strength of Solana is that it can process many more transactions per seconds than Ethereum and it also charges lower transaction fees. Additionally, Solana utilizes both proof-of-stake (PoS) and proof-of-work (PoW) models.

Name: Avalanche
Description: Avalanche is an open-sourced, smart contract blockchain that focuses on transaction speed, eco-friendliness and scalability. Due to its unique structure enabling subnets for each protocol on the chain, Avalanche is by far one of the fastest layer one chains in the crypto universe.

Name: Terra
Description: Terra LUNA is the most successful chain which powers the algorithmic stablecoin known as UST. LUNA itself is the other native token of the open-sourced blockchain known as Terra. LUNA is a cryptoasset with many functions: It's primary use besides being tradeable is it can be used as collateral for the Terra economy or used for governance and mining. Unlike other layer one protocols, Terra LUNA enables transactions to be paid in multiple coins (a unique feature other chains have not followed upon).

Name: Polkadot
Description: Polkadot is a network with multiple blockchains where users can transfer every type of data and asset—including tokens, DApps…—from one blockchain to another. Spreading transactions across many parallel blockchain also improves scalability.

Name: Dogecoin
Description: Originally created as a joke, Dogecoin works as a decentralized, peer-to-peer digital currency. It is considered the first ‘meme coin’ and has gathered for itself a large community of fans and investors. Dogecoin’s success is in great parts thanks to the endorsement of Reddit and Elon Musk.

Name: Shiba Inu
Description: Shiba Inu is an Ethereum-based token that is referred to by some as the ‘Dogecoin killer’. The token is “an experiment in decentralized spontaneous community building”, which means the project started from zero without a preassembled team or direction, and it’s 100% up to the community to build something out of it.

Name: Polygon
Description: Polygon is a layer 2 scaling solution working on top of the Ethereum blockchain. What Polygon brings to the table is faster and lower-cost transactions than Ethereum, and these transactions are also irreversible on the Ethereum mainchain.

Name: Cronos
Description: Cronos is the utility token of Cronos EVM Chain and Crypto.org Chain. The Cronos EVM Chain supports DeFi apps and allows users to port smart contracts and DApps from Ethereum and all EVM compatible chains. Crypto.com itself offers many services like wallet and card app, MCO Visa card,... with the purpose of accelerating the transition of the tradfi economy  into the native cryptocurrency landscape.

Name: Litecoin
Description: Litecoin is a decentralized internet currency founded 2 years after Bitcoin by former Google engineer Charlie Lee. Litecoin is used to transfer payment around the world with near-zero cost and fast transaction speed. Although it has lagged in development of other chains, it recently deployed an upgrade with the privacy oriented Mimble Wimble which gives it equal footing with other chains dedicated to anonymous transactions.

Name: Cosmos
Description: Cosmos is dubbed an “Internet of blockchains” by its founding team. It aims to use open-source tools to streamline transactions between different crypto networks. Cosmos focuses on customizability and interoperability with a goal to create an ecosystem of decentralized networks. Cosmos’s own PoS blockchain is called Cosmos Hub and is powered by ATOM. Many other chains have used the Cosmos SDK to buid out their own networks and is considered a layer zero chain in many theoretical circles.

Name: Chainlink
Description: Chainlink acting as a price oracle provides real-world data to smart contracts. Since most smart contracts can’t execute their terms without relying on external data sources, Chainlink incentivizes data providers to act as a bridge between the two. There are reputation scores and rewards assigned to each provider to further encourage them to provide accurate and useful data.

Name: Near Protocol
Description: NEAR protocol is a proof-of-stake (PoS) layer 1 blockchain that utilizes a sharding solution called ‘Nightshade’. This solution only lets each node to store a small subset of the platform’s data, which reduces potential points of failure security-wise. NEAR protocol also includes ‘Rainbow Bridge’, an application that lets users easily transfer Ethereum tokens to NEAR and vice versa.

Name: Tron
Description: The Tron blockchain has 3 layers to its architecture: the Core layer sends coded instructions to the Tron Virtual Machine; the Application layer lets developers create wallets and applications powered by TRX; and the Storage layer stores records of the blockchain’s history and status of smart contracts.

Name: FTX Token
Description: FTT Token is the native token of the FTX exchange, which is a centralized cryptocurrency exchange focused on derivatives, volatility products and leveraged products. FTX can be used as collateral, to get discounts on trading fees and more.

Name: Algorand
Description:
# Algorand Market Analysis

![Algorand went live in 2019](https://images.surferseo.art/70c51dee-3f6b-420c-ae15-1c69225458b6.jpeg)

## What is Algorand?

Going live in 2019, Algorand is a layer 1 decentralized blockchain technology that uses the Pure Proof of Stake (Pure PoS/ PPoS) consensus protocol and ALGO is its native currency. Algorand claims to be the best solution for solving the blockchain trilemma: achieving decentralization, scalability, and security.

The name comes from Algo (algorithm) and Rand (random): an algorithm that uses randomness to enhance the consensus mechanism. Algorand is primarily targeting the smart contract niche, with an emphasis on financial services, decentralized finance (DeFi), financial securities markets, and all sectors related to the future of finance.

## What is the Pure Proof of Stake Consensus Protocol?

Pure Proof of Stake (PPoS) is the conception of Algorand founder MIT professor Silvio Micali and is argued to lead to a more decentralized network compared to the existing Proof of Stake (PoS) protocol and be more resource efficient than Proof of Work.

In PPoS, validators are chosen to participate based on both the number of coins they own, but also through random selection, regardless of the size of their position.

This random selection of a 'committee' (participants that can add blocks to the network) is carried out by what Miclai describes as the 'secret sauce' of Algorand; Cryptographic Sortition. We can visualize Cryptographic Sortition as a weighted lottery, with each ALGO held acting as the lottery ticket. Due to the selection of the committee member being unpredictable, it is not possible to control them in advance, leading to a more secure network. And having a wider pool of participants to choose from also leads to greater decentralization. Cryptographic Sortition has been adopted by other next-generation blockchains like [Cardano](https://coinrotator.app/coin/cardano), will full credit given to Silvio Micali's protocol.

![Algorand claims to solve the Blockchain Trilema](https://images.surferseo.art/479465d1-7f29-4712-a1bc-276c6d2d8dc5.jpeg)

##   
What Problem Does Algorand Solve?

Thanks to the PPoS protocol which allows for high transaction throughput, the Algorand blockchain is hoping to be the preferred chain for Defi, as well as the exchange of digital property or NFTs. Algorand also bills itself as the ‘greenest’ blockchain. Since 2021, Algorand has been ‘carbon neutral’; a portion of the transaction fees goes towards the purchase of Carbon Credit, which offsets its carbon emissions.

### Algorand State Proofs

Algorands recent protocol upgrade saw the introduction of Algorand State Proofs (ASP); Algorands new interoperability standard that allows interoperability with all Proof-of-Stake blockchains. Previous solutions to the interoperability problem relied on trusted intermediaries like centralized bridges or validator networks. These create single points of failure or attack vectors that allow malicious parties to compromise the system. ASPs allow the creation of a low-powered, lightweight 'snapshot' of the 'state' of the blockchain (current balances, transactions, or application data) to be read by the interoperated blockchain. Due to their low-powered nature, they can be used in environments such as a phone, smartwatch, or inside a smart contract. All Proof-of-Stake blockchains will be able to use State Proofs. State Proofs will be generated with advanced **Falcon Keys**, which ensures security even when faced with an attack from advanced Quantum Computers.

![MIT professor Silvio Micali founded Algorand](https://images.surferseo.art/c0a481b0-23fd-4150-b6c2-fc81256d3e52.jpeg)

## Who is the Team Behind ALGO?

The powerhouse behind the creation of the Algorand network is MIT professor Silvio Micali, who has a very high standing in the cryptocurrency and blockchain space. He has over 40 years of expertise behind him and has contributed to some of the most well-known cryptocurrency advancements including public-key cryptosystems, pseudorandom functions, digital signatures, and oblivious transfer, and is one of the co-inventors of Zero-Knowledge Proofs and Verifiable Random Functions, which have been adopted by many third generation blockchains such as [Ethereum](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ethereum), [Cardano](https://coinrotator.app/coin/cardano), and [Polkadot](https://coinrotator.app/coin/polkadot).

He has gone on to receive many rewards such as the Gödel Prize, the RSA Award for Excellence in Mathematics (2004), and the Turing Award (2012) for his work in the field of cryptography. He is a member of the National Academy of Sciences, the National Academy of Engineering, the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, and a fellow of the International Association for Cryptologic Research.

In the senior leadership team, Micali is joined by interim CEO W.Sean Ford, the previous CMO for LogMeIn. He brings expertise in brand leadership, product marketing, global marketing strategy, and e-commerce. Paul Riegle joined Algorand from Carbon Black where he led product management for initiatives that produced over $200million in recurring revenue.

## Road Map and Future Plans for Algorand (ALGO).

Algorand doesn’t technically have a roadmap but upcoming milestones can be ascertained from interviews, presentations, and publications from the Algorand team.

In July 2022 the Algorand research team published their report titled “Issuing Central Bank Digital Currency Using Algorand”. The reports suggest a hybrid CBDC model using a private instance of a public blockchain such as Algorand. In an interview last year COO Sean Ford confirmed they are in talks with Central Banks around the world to build their CBDC infrastructure but didn't specify which ones.

Algorands main priority is to establish itself in the competitive smart contract market niche to attract market share away from competitors such as Ethereum, whose users often complain of high gas fees. With Next-Generation scaling technologies, Algorand can scale to 10,000 transactions per second, which even dwarfs traditional finance networks such as Visa. With this high scalability and smart contract functionality, Algorand Defi has recently launched products including decentralized exchanges, predictions markets, lending protocols, automated market makers, and more.

## Current Partnerships in the Algorand (ALGO) Ecosystem.

Algorand has over 500 active partnerships with global organizations, governments, and d[efi applications](https://coinrotator.app/?category=DeFi). They are active in finance, education, gaming, sports and music, and arts fields.

### Algorand FIFA

In the sports sector, they recently partnered to form Algorand FIFA, to become a FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022™ Regional Supporter. Algorand will assist with their digital assets strategy, while FIFA will provide sponsorship assets including advertising, media exposure as well as promotional opportunities.  

### Algorand Foundation's Global University Program

Thanks to Dr. Micali’s connection to MIT, Algorand has been able to form strong partnerships with top-ranking universities around the world. The Algorand Foundation's Global University Program was formed to allow students and faculty from these prestigious institutions the opportunity to help grow the network and contribute to the new borderless knowledge economy.

![Algorand Global University Program](https://images.surferseo.art/31454902-8ada-4146-8626-365d9bfd8abb.jpeg)

## Algorand Defi

In the financial securities arena, Algorand has formed working collaborations with Republic; the private investment and technology platform, Archax; the FCA authorized digital securities exchange, Stoneize; the Italian fintech, Securitize; the compliance platform and Delchain; the asset management firm.

## Algorand Governance

An important distinction between Algorand and other blockchains is that Algorand rewards Governance, not Consensus. Validators are incentivized to run nodes to support their investment in the ecosystem. In fact, due to the minimal computational costs, and the voluntary nature of the action, consensus participation is seen as "a very light form of civic service", according to Micali. Governance, on the other hand, will require personal attention and time, in addition to a locking up period of the Algorand token, and as such will be compensated. Governance is defined as the power of deciding any non-consensus task, for example, the funding of grant proposals. This decentralized governance model will give Algorand coin holders and the Algorand community the opportunity to participate in the ecosystem’s decision-making process on the Algorand Platform.

![Algorand rewards Governance not Consensus ](https://images.surferseo.art/54d27f9e-77a2-411b-b8ff-7b71586a1606.jpeg)

## The Tokenomics of ALGO

Algorand cryptocurrency has a total supply of 10 billion. The circulating supply currently sits at {{circulatingsupply}} ({{percentagecirculatingsupply}}). The remaining ALGOs are held by the Algorand Foundation in secure wallets and earmarked for Community & Governance Rewards, Ecosystem Support, and the Foundation Endowment. As of {{month}} {{day}} {{year}} ALGO is trading at {{currentprice}} with a marketcap of {{marketcap}}.

### Algorand Price Predictions

To get a general possible Algorand price forecast we can look at past token price history. As stated the ALGO price currently sits at {{currentprice}}. A rise back to all-time highs of {{ath}} would see a near 700% appreciation in the ALGO price, and some chartists see this as a minimum price target if the overall crypto market cap re-visits the 2021 highs at $3Trillion. Although forming an Algorand price prediction with the current level of market uncertainty is difficult.

This price level may be seen as a good investment for investors with a long-term horizon. Please note that this isn't investment advice and traders and investors should do their own research.

## Where to Buy Algorand

Algorand coins can be bought on all major cryptocurrency exchanges including Coinbase, Binance, Bitrue, Kucoin, and Huobi.

![Algorand - a carbon neutral blockchain](https://images.surferseo.art/8c97c596-600a-4a14-a39a-16918f61d608.jpeg)

Name: Fantom
Description: FTM is a layer 1 decentralized smart contract platform aimed at greatly reducing transaction speed and scalability of blockchain technology while also maintaining high security. Utilizing DAG technology, FTM reduces the time needed for transaction, ensures exchanges occur instantly, and handles thousands of transactions every second.

Name: Stellar
Description: Stellar is an open-source, decentralized platform for fiat money that allows easy transactions between any pair of currencies. Stellar is designed to be able to work with all of the world's financial systems. It also aims to vastly improve transaction speed and reduce the cost of transactions while maintaining high security. XLM is a decentralized fork of the XRP blockchain.

Name: Uniswap
Description: Uniswap is one of the first and largest decentralized exchanges that is hosted on the Ethereum blockchain. Uniswap pioneered the Automated Market Maker system, which uses a liquidity pool instead of cryptocurrency exchanges run by other centralized companies. Using the liquidity pool, Uniswap enables peer-to-peer exchanges to happen effortlessly, allowing anyone, from anywhere, to trade.

Name: Hedera
Description:
# Hedera Hashgraph Analysis

![Hedera Hashgraph was created in 2016](https://images.surferseo.art/11eda7a6-6785-4c37-af03-890362026ed7.jpeg)

## How did Hedera Hashgraph Start Out?

The Hashgraph was created and patented in 2016 by Swirlds CEO, Dr. Leemon Baird as a faster and more scalable alternative to existing distributed ledger technology (DLT) protocols.

The Hedera Hashgraph cryptocurrency describes itself as "the most used, sustainable, enterprise-grade public network for the decentralised economy".

It was first aimed at the financial and corporate sector, allowing greater transaction and security using the application of the directed acyclic graph (DAG).

The Hashgraph was initially chosen as the DLT of choice for a consortium of 6000 banks looking to create an interoperable network.

This first interest led to the creation of the Hedera Hashgraph Platform, which can be seen as analogues to the [Ethereum](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ethereum) network, albeit without traditional blockchain technology and a differing management model.  

## Blockchain Vs. Hashgraph Technology

![Dr. Leemon Baird Co-Creator of Hashgraph](https://images.surferseo.art/267527fd-2f4e-43d6-a79f-d97bd78a8e59.png)

### Blockchain Technology

A first-generation Proof-of-Work (PoW) Blockchain such as [Bitcoin](https://coinrotator.app/coin/bitcoin) operates in a linear fashion, meaning all the nodes, the participants of the network, collect all the transactions that are flowing into the network and arrange them into ‘blocks’.

Each ‘block’ contains the transaction information, a timestamp and a cryptographic ‘hash’ of the previous block (the hash converts the data into a fixed string of random digits). All this data is generated into a further hash which is linked to the next block forming a ‘chain’.

The ‘nodes’ then have to compete with one another, and consensus (agreement) has to be reached as to which block will be the next in the chain.

This process can take a lot of energy to process, and it can be slow, as all of the nodes have to reach consensus, so some see it as inefficient.

![Blockchain Vs. Hashgraph](https://images.surferseo.art/84d86d4a-3e73-450b-9a7d-88060ccf1f1e.jpeg)

### Hedera Hashgraph Network Technology

![Hedera Hashgraph DAGs allow for near instantaneous and exponential sharing of information](https://images.surferseo.art/18a425e0-7100-4df8-bbb7-0c224e72d65b.png)

Compare this to the Hashgraph: in very simple terms, as the transactions flow into the network, the nodes arrange them into directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) (think of an ancestry tree compared to a linear chain of block) which are then quickly time segmented and shared with other nodes using the Hashgraph consensus algorithm; the Gossip About Gossip Protocol.

This allows for near-instantaneous and exponential sharing of information; nodes communicate at random all the information they hold to other nodes, similar to how a virus or pathogen might spread in a target population.

Each time two nodes successfully share information, including a time stamp, transaction detail, previous hashes, and a cryptographic signature, an 'event' is created, as opposed to a 'block' on a blockchain.

The history of events, and corresponding hashes, takes the form of a graph, or a graph of hashes; a Hashgraph. Thanks to this protocol, nodes can determine in seconds where a transaction should be placed in the history of the ledger. Auitable, decentralised logs of 'events' are created by the Hedera Consensus Service, and made available for Web2 and Web3 applications.

![Hedera Consensus Service create decentralised logs of 'events' for Web3 applications](https://images.surferseo.art/b3c6c813-23a5-4aa9-8da6-85f1fa31f312.webp)

## Hedera Hashgraph Platform Governance structure.

At the top of the governance, hierarchy is the Hedera Governing Council, comprised of 39 members made up of private and legal entities, distributed among various industries and geographical regions.

These entities will decide the development and direction of the project. Current council members include Google, IBM, BOEING, DBS, LG, UBISOFT, among others.

![Hedera Hashgraph Governing Council](https://images.surferseo.art/1c699a1a-8e33-4f6c-acab-06e3b364e426.png)

## The Hedera HBAR Token

HBAR is the native token of the public Hedera Network. It has two core uses; Network Fuel and Network Protection.

### Network Fuel

HBAR tokens are needed by developers for services such as minting NFTs and tokens, calling smart contracts, logging data and transferring HBAR on the network.

### Network Protection

HBAR is needed to protect the network from attack via Proof-of-Stake (PoS). HBAR stakes on the network have a weighted influence for transaction validation consensus. Weighted voting makes it unlikely a malicious actor could affect consensus.

### Hedera HBAR Tokenomics

The current market cap is {{marketcap}}. There is a maximum supply of 50 billion HBAR, no more can be created or minted. At the time of writing the circulating supply is {{circulatingsupply}}, which represents {{percentagecirculatingsupply}} of the total supply.

The remaining tokens are held at the treasury of the Hedera Hashgraph, LLC, which is overseen by the Governing Council.

These coins are used for staking and are on a slow release schedule. Hedera explains the large concentration of coins held as being necessary to secure the network. An attacker could disrupt the network by owning or controlling a ⅓ share of the coins.

Hedera anticipates that “the released supply of HBARs will remain below 10% of the total HBAR supply for the first year and below 33% of the total HBAR supply for four-to-five years”, according to their website.

![Hedera Hashgraph partners ](https://images.surferseo.art/ac19648f-e49d-4411-b4b9-f62593dbeb52.png)

## Partners and Future Direction of Hedera Hashgraph HBAR

### Hedera Hashgraph's Banking and Finance Partnerships

Hedera has been active in cementing inroads in the banking and finance sector. In addition to the existing banks on the Governance Council, Africa's largest bank by assets; Standard Bank Group, recently partnered with Hedera Hashgraph, with the aim of reducing settlement periods and bottlenecks on their network, in addition to helping to increase transaction transparency.

### Hedera Hashgraph CBDC

Rumors are circulating in the crypto space about a possible working collaboration between Hedera and an upcoming Federal Reserve-backed Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC).

Hedera was first earmarked as a possible candidate for hosting a CBDC back in 2019 after India's National Institute for Smart Government (NISG) published a draft of its National Strategy on Blockchain, specifically referencing Hedera.

### Hedera Hashgraph Healthcare

In healthcare, Hedera Hashgraph has forged a partnership with the UK's medical tech firm Everyware. The arrangement would see Hedera Hashgraph keep a tamper-proof digital record of temperature-sensitive COVID vaccines and pick up on any storage anomalies.

![Hedera Hashgraph partners with UK tech firm Everyware ](https://images.surferseo.art/ffccd47b-6b43-4615-a7ce-4ae02691df12.jpeg)![ HBAR Hedera token service](https://images.surferseo.art/27c66034-6435-41fb-9d86-5d3350936214.jpeg)

### What is a Possible Hedera Hashgraph Price Prediction?

Hedera Hashgraph price forecast; as for HBAR price analysis and possible HBAR price prediction, at the time of writing the current price is {{currentprice}}. This marks a roughly {{percentagefromath}}retrace from its all-time high price of {{ath}}, a drawdown that has been seen throughout the overall cryptocurrency market. HBAR is below its ICO price {{launchprice}} which is unlikely to remain once bear market conditions clear up. If HBAR was able to succeed in becoming a top 10 coin a minimum price of $3 could be reached, if the overall cryptocurrency market resumes its bullish sentiment, which would be comparable to other coins of a similar ranking and supply.

Therefore some traders see this current Hedera price as an opportunity for a good investment permitting them to have a long-term time horizon. Please make note, Hedera Hashgraph price predictions are often uncertain. Traders and investors should do their own research and this is not intended as financial advice.

### Where can we Buy Hedera Hashgraph's HBAR coins?

Those looking to buy Hedera Hashgraph can find the digital asset listed on most of the main exchanges including Binance, Kucoin, Upbit and Bitrue.

Name: Ethereum Classic
Description: Ethereum is a decentralized, open-sourced platform that utilizes smart contracts which ensures speed, transparency, and security. After the DAO incident in 2016, Ethereum split into Ethereum and Ethereum Classic. The Classic version maintains unchanged history and is secured from external attacks on transactions.

Name: Internet Computer
Description: Internet Computer is a public blockchain network that utilizes smart contracts. Initially released in 2019 by Dfinity, Internet Computer is a  “general-purpose” blockchain that provides a public platform for hosting tokens and decentralized applications.Their ultimate goal is to create a decentralized internet to power the worlds decentralized apps (dApps).

Name: Decentraland
Description: Decentraland is a virtual-based platform where players can create and monetize virtual plots of land as NFT through the use of the MANA cryptocurrency. Decentraland aims at creating an immersive virtual world in the metaverse where users can socialize, interact, learn and play. Decentraland is a decentralized world where users can explore land owned by other users or trade virtual assets such as NFTs.

Name: Axie Infinity
Description: Axie Infinity tokens are ERC-20 tokens used in the Axie Infinity Universe. A minimum of 3 Axie Infinity tokens is required to begin playing and earning from the Axie Infinity Universe. AXS is mainly used for in-game purchases such as other users' "Axies" (creatures that can be collected, bred, and battled), or plots of lands and other items in the form of NFTs.

Name: Vechain
Description: VeChain is a public blockchain platform that aims at improving supply chain management and business procedures. Vechain's main token is denoted as VET, an ERC-20 on the Ethereum blockchain. VeChain aims at connecting blockchain technology to the world by providing infrastructures and IOT (Internet of things) integration.

Name: Osmosis
Description: Osmosis is a decentralized, open-source, peer-to-peer blockchain primarily used as an AMM (Automated Market Maker). Osmosis adopts the Inter-Blockchain-Communication (IBC) that allows fast and cost-effective connection to the Osmosis network called SDK. By using Osmosis, you can create liquidity and trade IBC-enabled tokens.

Name: Filecoin
Description: Filecoin is a public cryptocurrency and digital payment system intended to be a blockchain-based method of co-operating digital storage and retrieval. Filecoin is an open protocol and backed by a blockchain that records commitments made by the network's participants, with transactions recorded in FIL, the blockchain's native currency.

Name: Monero
Description: Monero is a decentralized cryptocurrency. Unlike other cryptocurrencies, Monero boasts privacy features. Monero's blockchain cannot track or trace transactions. To create new coins and secure transactions, Monero uses a proof of work consensus algorithm.

Name: Elrond
Description: A distributed transactional computation protocol built on the Elrond concept, Elrond uses a secure Proof of Stake consensus method and sharded state architecture. Elrond runs on ordinary computers, contrary to most blockchain networks, which use custom hardware and high energy consumption. Using the sharding method, Elrond's performance will scale with the number of computers joining the network.

Name: Klaytn
Description: Klaytn is a public blockchain platform built by Kakao, South Korea's most popular mobile platform. Using an efficient 'hybrid' design, it combines the advantages of public blockchain ( decentralized data & control, distributed governance), and private blockchains (decentralized control, distributed governance).

Name: Theta Network
Description: Theta Network is a decentralized video delivery network that integrates blockchain technology as well as users' computer power to improve video quality. Theta aims at bolstering video quality and speed of video delivery in countries where the infrastructure required is not up to the task. In exchange for users' contributions to the distribution network, they will be rewarded with THETA tokens.

Name: Tezos
Description: Tezos is a decentralized, open-source blockchain that can deploy smart contracts and allow peer-to-peer transactions. Tezos utilizes liquidity proof-of-stake, which allows it to adjust itself automatically when its upgrade proposal gets enough votes from the community.

Name: Magic Internet Money
Description: Abracadabra decentralized money lending platform's Magic Internet Money is an ERC - 20 token and is soft-pegged to the US Dollar. MIM is backed by interest-bearing tokens and can be traded with other stablecoins.

Name: Ecomi
Description: ECOMI is a business registered in Singapore. Through its app, VeVe, a digital collectible platform built based on blockchain, ECOMI functions as a one-stop-shop for digital collectibles. ECOMI aims at bringing pop culture into the 21st century. For users to interact with one another, as well as have a currency to trade and own digital collectibles, ECOMI uses tokens namely OMI.

Name: Helium
Description: Helium is a decentralized machine networking technology. It operates on a peer-to-peer wireless network, which utilizes low-powered devices with an Internet connection to deliver data to and from the Internet. Miners earn HNT by utilizing hardware called a Hotspot to provide network coverage and relay data over the internet.

Name: Bittorent [OLD]
Description: BTT is an RTC - 10 utility token originated from Bittorent, the world's biggest decentralized, peer-to-peer file-sharing network. Bittorent aims to incentivize seeding, as well as dedicating band width and storage space by allowing downloaders to exchange BTT to seeders for faster download speed.

Name: The Graph
Description: The Graph protocol, like networks such as Ethereum and IPFS, is an indexing protocol used to query data, enabling many applications both within DeFi itself, which all are part of the broader Web3 ecosystem. Using GRT, APIs (aplication programing interface) can be built and published by anyone. These are called subgraphs that can query using GraphQL to retrieve blockchain data.

Name: EOS
Description: EOS is a decentralized blockchain that is dependent on its own currency, the EOS token. EOS supports decentralized applications called DApps (decentralized application) on its platform. It utilizes smart contracts to form fast and seamless transactions as well as significantly reduce the cost while maintaining millions of transactions every second.

Name: IOTA
Description: IOTA is based on a distributed ledger technology called "The Tangle". The Tangle remains similar to blockchain but instead exchanges the block structure for directed acyclic graph structure. While requiring a small proof-of-work during the transaction from users, it removes miners and transaction fees entirely, with the only cost being paid by users in the proof-of-work process.

Name: Waves
Description: Waves is a digital currency platform that allows users to create their own cryptocurrencies. Waves focus on inter-chain DeFi, so every token created on Waves can be traded with one another. Waves platform comprises 3 layers: Protocol layer, Infrastructure layer, Application layer.

Name: Juno
Description: Juno is a decentralized, permission-less public blockchain in the Cosmos ecosystem that provides a sandbox for developing interoperable smart contracts. JUNO is the native token of the platform which is used to secure the consensus layer, fuel smart contract execution, or capture value from DApps and smart contracts built on the Juno network.

Name: Harmony
Description: Harmony is a blockchain-based platform for developing and using decentralized applications (DApps). By focusing on random state sharding, which enables the creation of blocks in seconds, the network intends to reinvent the way decentralized applications work. Harmony focuses on processing speed and validation to revolutionize block creation. With the sharding process, Harmony massively reduces the validation time for each node.

Name: OKB
Description: OKB is a token introduced by OK Blockchain Foundation, with a purpose to build an OKEx ecosystem to accelerate the development of blockchain technology and digital asset industry. The acceleration is achieved by using OKB to connect users, investors and digital assets projects.

Name: PancakeSwap
Description: CAKE token is issued by PancakeSwap, a decentralized exchange specializing in swapping BEP-20 tokens, which are tokens built on the Binance Smart Chain that don’t have their own blockchain. PancakeSwap has one of the largest daily user counts of any dApp and it can also connect to many different wallets. CAKE can be used for staking with an impressively stable but high APY.

Name: Flow
Description: After their NFT token launch rendering the Ethereum blockchain almost unusable due to traffic jam, the creators behind CryptoKitties created a blockchain they hope would solve this problem. The Flow playground, therefore, is a place for developers to create and trade NFTs; but it is a blockchain with a multi-node architecture (collection nodes, consensus nodes, execution nodes and verification nodes), each node handling separate responsibilities in order to maximize speed.

Name: Gala
Description: In a market saturated with many opportunist cash-grabs, Gala Games aims to be a blockchain games studio that not only creates games that are enjoyable to play but players can also take ownership of their in-game items.

Name: Aave
Description: Aave is a DeFi project working as a lending protocol that users can lend, borrow and earn interest on 17 different types of crypto assets. No middleman involved, Aave runs on a system of smart contracts, using collaterals as security for the loans. In addition, Aave allows for special features such as flash loans.

Name: Maker
Description: Maker is a protocol which maintains DAI, a stablecoin designed to track the price of the US dollars, using other crypto assets. MKR is the governance token of the Maker protocol so MKR holders can vote in the MakerDAO to make changes to the protocol, such as which assets can be locked in the protocol and how much would they sell for in liquidation.

Name: Theta Fuel
Description: Theta Fuel (TFUEL) is one of the 2 native tokens running on the Theta blockchain, the other being THETA. While THETA is the governance token, TFUEL is used as a utility token and to execute transactions. TFUEL is the gas coin that powers all functions of the Theta blockchain.

Name: Bitcoin SV
Description: BSV is the native cryptocurrency of the blockchain called Bitcoin Satoshi’s Vision, which is a hard fork version of the Bitcoin Cash blockchain. Bitcoin Cash itself is another hard fork of the original Bitcoin blockchain. Bitcoin SV aims to “restore the original Satoshi protocol, keep it stable and enable it to massively scale.”

Name: Quant
Description:
# Complete Quant (QNT) Analysis

>![Quant Network launched in 2018](https://images.surferseo.art/7857c3bd-60bc-40e9-ac3f-4e689e9cc91a.jpeg)
> 
> "To unlock the power of blockchain for everyone, by delivering interoperable ecosystems and real-world solutions that simply work: lowering costs, enabling new business and mitigating risk." - **Quant Network; Mission and Value**

## What is QNT Network?

Quant launched in 2018 and offers a unique solution to the interoperability problem. The company founders highlighted the inability of blockchains to interoperate with both different blockchains and existing legacy networks as the main hurdle to wider blockchain adoption.

Billed as the first interoperable Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) blockchain Operating System (OS), Quant’s Overledger created a plug-and-play solution to this problem which helps bridge the gap between blockchain and enterprise software, using a simple API interface that doesn't require any additional infrastructure or blockchain development expertise.

The Overledger DLT Gateway allows enterprises and developers to link their preexisting infrastructure to other DLTs and build multi DLT applications (mDApps) which can access and execute on all underlying networks simultaneously. The Quant Overledger development tools are enterprise-grade built, for connecting enterprises, regulatory bodies and public organisations.

## Network Ecosystem

The complete Quant ecosystem is comprised of the following components;

-   **Overledger OS**
    
-   **Multi-DLT applications (mDApps)**
    
-   **Overledger Network**
    
-   **Overledger Network Marketplace**
    
-   **The Treasury**
    
-   **The Quant token**
    

### **Overledger OS**

Overledger Operating System (OS) is the core engine. What is now the world's first blockchain agnostic API gateway Operating System, allowing users to connect with multiple DLTs, other Blockchains, Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) or other legacy networks simultaneously via a simple-to-use standardized REST API interface. The Overledger OS license will allow organisations interoperability between internal DLTs. Think of Overledger OS as Windows or Mac OS for the worlds DLTs.

### **Multi DLT Applications (mDApps)**

What can otherwise be described as “Multi-DLT smart contracts", are essentially smart contracts which can be built and run on multiple blockchains and DLTs, allowing decentralised applications to leverage the data and features of the underlying DLTs in ways not previously possible.

MDApps allow the user to leverage specific features of the underlying DLT, for example combining the speed of the Binance Smart Chain with the security of the Bitcoin blockchain to settle payments.

![Multi Chain Applications (mApps)](https://images.surferseo.art/7057e949-e968-4995-81d3-0b86e2810aad.jpeg)

### **Overledger Network**

The Overledger Network enables Overledger customers to host their own gateways to interoperate and unlock permissioned DLTs and private digital assets. Currently, Overledger Network supports the top enterprise blockchains including Ethereum, Bitcoin, Hyperledger Fabric, Corda, Quorum, [XRP](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ripple) Ledger and the [XDC](https://coinrotator.app/coin/xdce-crowd-sale) Network.

![Quant Overledger Network](https://images.surferseo.art/e10c0a7d-8fa0-41ab-8a00-2e77b8a052c2.png)

### **Overledger Network Marketplace**

The Overledger Network Marketplace is envisioned to allow participants of the Overledger Network to buy and sell data or digital applications (mDApps) that are derived from or run on the Quant Network ecosystem.

### **The Quant Treasury**

The Treasury handles fees paid by enterprise clients to access the network in addition to processing the transactions from the Overledger Network Marketplace.

Enterprise access to the Quant Network is priced and paid for in fiat but executed using QNT via smart contract. This QNT is held in the Treasury for the duration of the license.

## **The Quant Token**

The Quant erc-20 token has quite appealing tokenomics. Out of a total supply of {totalsupply}} }, {{circulatingsupply}} are currently circulating which represents ({{percentagecirculatingsupply}}) of the max supply. Demand for the token will come in several forms.

### **License Payment**

To access the Quant Network, enterprises, blockchain developers and gateway operators have to purchase annual licenses. The fee is paid in fiat and the subsequent amount in QNT will be locked up in smart contracts for the duration of the license (12 months) thereby further reducing the circulating supply. On expiration, the user will have the option to renew, which would see a further lockup of QNT, or it would otherwise be returned to the Treasury. As the Quant Network grows so will the demand grow for the Quant token.

### **Platform fees, Add-On-Services,Transaction fees**

Enterprise clients will need to pay platform fees, again paid in QNT and handled by the Treasury. Transaction fees from the mApps contracts will also be priced in QNT.

### **Multi Chain Oracles**

Data providers can choose to sell their off-chain data to the Overledger Network community. This will be paid for in QNT.

### **Quant Gateway Staking**

QNT will have to be locked by Gateway Operators when they process requests within the Overledger Network.

### **Signing of Transactions**

QNT will be needed to validate, sign and encrypt if necessary, all transactions within the Quant ecosystem.

## Who is the Team Behind QNT?

The Quant Network was the brainchild of current CEO Gilbert Verdian who founded the company in 2015. Verdian has a long and distinguished history working with banks, regulators and cyber security.

Previous positions were held at the UK's Ministry of Justice; the Bank of England’s CMORG Cyber Unit (2016) and on the Federal Reserve’s Secure Payment Task Force (2018). Verdian even established the ISO/TC 307 benchmark, which has the scope of the ‘Standardisation of blockchain technologies and distributed ledger technologies' according to the website.

![Quant CEO Gilbert Verdian](https://images.surferseo.art/53bba0b1-3057-46fd-ae70-26370e960bbd.jpeg)

## What are the Current and Future Partnerships of Quant Network?

The Quant team are known for keeping their cards close to their chest when it comes to partnerships, but the ones we know about are impressive.

Quant Network is currently partnered with Oracle, the world's largest database management company. LACCHAIN, a global alliance led by IDB Lab of the Inter-American Development Bank. SIA, now part of the Nexi Group, one of the worlds largest payment solutions groups.

In addition, Quant is in talks with Central Banks including the European Central Bank., the Federal Reserve, the Bank for International Settlements and finance regulatory bodies such as the UK's Financial Conduct Authority.

Quant are also members of the Digital Pound Foundation in the UK, tasked with the implementation of the Digital Pound, a Central Bank Digital Currency.

## QNT Main Competitors

Other projects that are attempting to address the interoperability problem include [Cosmos](https://coinrotator.app/coin/cosmos), [Polkadot](https://coinrotator.app/coin/polkadot), [AION](https://coinrotator.app/coin/aion), [ICON](https://coinrotator.app/coin/icon) as well as Ripple Lab's Interledger. Gilbert Verdian suggests there are limitations in these projects solving interoperability without adding additional complexities, technical risk and overheads. Verdian argues Overledger is best positioned to solve interoperability at the enterprise level. In a recent paper he stated:

_"Overledger is the_ **_only_** _platform to facilitate internet-scale development and deployment of decentralised, multi-chain applications (MApps) and Treaty Contracts (multi-chain smart contracts)_

_Overledger’s the only technology that allows for true N:N interoperability, meeting external security and regulatory requirements, and require minimal change to existing systems and networks to access the networks of blockchains through Overledger."_

## TLDR

In summary, Quant Network is a uniquely positioned blockchain project in the current crypto market. They had the foresight to envision that seamless interoperability was needed before mass adoption of blockchain technology and DLT was to occur. This included inter-blockchain communication, as well as integrating existing DLTs and enterprise systems. While still early in the game, Quant Networks' vision of full interoperability is fast becoming reality.

The Quant QNT coin is available on all major cryptocurrency exchanges including Binance, Coinbase and Kucoin.  As of {{month}} {{day}} {{year}} QNT is trading at {{currentprice}} 
  
Those investing with a long-term view could find the current price attractive, although the reader should do their own research and this is not intended as investment advice.

Name: Huobi Token
Description: A native token of the Huobi Global exchange, Huobi Token (hT) is a crypto asset built on the Ethereum blockchain and is ERC-20 compliant. HT can be used to reduce fees for trading operations on the exchange, as well as getting a monthly VIP status, receive crypto rewards for seasonal buybacks and vote on the exchange governance.

Name: Arweave
Description: Arweave specializes in storing data, working as a decentralized storage network and describing itself as “a collectively owned hard drive that never forgets”. Arweave hosts a permanent and decentralized website containing community-driven applications and platforms, using AR to pay miners to maintain the network.

Name: Zcash
Description: In short, Zcash is a Bitcoin fork that prioritizes privacy. Transactions on Zcash are designed to be completely anonymous by using the Zerocash protocol to hide the sender, recipient and amount of the transactions. However, users can still choose the transparent transaction option: this is mainly for accommodating wallets or exchanges that demand transparency.

Name: Neo
Description: Neo is a platform that enables developers to design dapps that aim to mimic real world products and services. Neo uses NeoContracts instead of normal smart contracts, which lets users build applications with already pre-established languages like C# and Java. Moreover, NEO owners can vote to help operate the Neo network.

Name: KuCoin Token
Description: KuCoin is a cryptocurrency exchange, allowing users to buy, sell and store a wide variety of crypto assets. KCS, the native token of the exchange, can be used to reduce fees and earn rewards. You can create and use a KuCoin account with just an email address but to access the advanced features, the account needs to be verified. Some of the features in KuCoin are trading bots, leverage, margin…

Name: eCash
Description: eCash used to be known as Bitcoin Cash ABC (BCHA): a fork of Bitcoin (BTC) and Bitcoin Cash (BCH). eCash aims to be a virtual currency that will be actually used to pay for goods and services. Since its rebrand, eCash has introduced “bits” as its base unit, so you would send 10 bits with eCash compared to 0.00001000 BTC.

Name: Humans.ai
Description: Humans.ai aims to create an all-in-one platform for ethical AI-based creations and governance. The initial focus for Humans.ai is synthetic media, and using its token-based ownership and accountability system, the platform ensures that the AIs are uncorrupted long-term.

Name: THORchain
Description: THORchain is a decentralized liquidity protocol, which means it helps people transfer their crypto assets across a variety of networks without losing full custody of the assets. Users can utilize THORchain to swap assets with comfort in a permissionless setting. RUNE is the native token of the platform, used for governance, security and as the base token in the THORchain ecosystem.

Name: Enjin Coin
Description: Enjin Coin is an ERC-20 token built on the Ethereum network which fuels the Enjin ecosystem. It’s one of the largest gaming community platforms online with over 250,000 gaming communities and more than 18 million registered gamers. Enjin brings its value to the community with its seamless creation and management of virtual goods by directly backing the value of blockchain assets.

Name: NEXO
Description: Nexo is a lending platform for cryptocurrencies powered and backed by Credissimo - a leading FinTech Group established in 2007. The platform currently supports over 40 different currencies and has been operating since 2018.

Name: Amp
Description: AMP provides straightforward but versatile means of verifiable collateralization, through a system of collateral partitions and collateral managers. A partition can serve as insurance for essentially anything of value. These can range from accounts, applications, to even transactions. Assets are locked, released, or redirected as necessary to support value transfer activities.

Name: Celcious Network
Description: CEL is an ERC-20 token and the native currency of the Celsius Network. A centralized network that allows people to lend and borrow cryptocurrencies using a peer-to-peer lending platform. You’ll be able to earn interest on a loan or take a loan yourself, all in your desired currency.

Name: Radix
Description: Radix is the first decentralized layer one protocol specifically built to serve and improve DeFi. It’s suitable for thousands of projects to build, scale and deploy with close to unlimited financial applications. Radix has developed its own programming language called Scrypto, designed to facilitate the creation of decentralized financial applications (dApps).

Name: Celo
Description: The CELO cryptocurrency serves both as a utility token and as a governance token for the blockchain platform Celo. Powered by Proof-of-Stake (PoS) protocol, the blockchain provides users the ability to participate and earn rewards on the platform while supporting stablecoins and tokenized assets with an algorithmic reserve mechanism.

Name: Kusama
Description: Kusama is an early and highly experimental version of Polkadot that represents real-world economic conditions. Kusama is considered the real-money testnest of Polkadot which is not economically centralized and will continue to exist as long as it is maintained by the community. The team aims to cater to innovative early-stage development and deployment of new applications.

Name: Stacks
Description: Stacks is a decentralized computing network and app ecosystem that uses STX as its native currency. It’s used to fuel smart contracts for Bitcoin and rewards miners on the open Stacks network, by allowing holders to earn BTC with stacking. Applications built on Stacks inherit all the benefits of Bitcoin. In this way, decentralized apps can exist as a kind of layer two of BTC.

Name: Kadena
Description: KDA is a cryptocurrency used to pay for computational work in the Kadena public chain. In the same way that Ethereum miners receive ETH for mining blocks, Kadena miners are paid in KDA for mining blocks. It’s one of the world’s first truly scalable blockchains that are ready for applications. Several major companies have already implemented this enterprise software, including those in healthcare, finance, and insurance and carbon credit exchanges.

Name: Dash
Description: Dash is a fork of Bitcoin established in 2014 and is an acronym for “digital cash”. It is one of the oldest and longest-running decentralized autonomous organizations (DAO). Its focus is on transactions speed and privacy. With Dash, you can remain anonymous while making transactions, just like with cash. Payments are easy to make, instant, secure, and essentially free.

Name: Chiliz
Description: Chiliz is a fintech platform that aims to tokenize the sports and entertainment sectors by creating a fan engagement platform. The project essentially gives eSports and sports fans the ability to crowd-manage their favorite leagues, games, and teams. Many Premier League Teams has already issued tokens on the Chiliz network.

Name: Frax Share
Description: Frax Share is the governance token for the Frax protocol, which uses an algorithmic stablecoin system to provide decentralized and scalable money. It is designed to be volatile and to hold the rights to governance as well as all excess collateral. Furthermore, it incorporates the same ethos of Bitcoin in designing trustless money based on a highly reduced governance structure.

Name: Anchor Protocol
Description: ANC is Anchor Protocol's governance token, built and backed by Terraform Labs. It is a decentralized lending protocol on the Tera cryptocurrency blockchain. Using the protocol, a portion of Anchor's yield can be captured, so its value increases linearly with Anchor's assets. Powered by Proof of Stake returns across multiple blockchains, Anchor's yield is stable and attractive.

Name: Basic Attention Token
Description: Basic Attention Token (BAT) is an Ethereum based token specifically designed to be used with the Brave browser in order to facilitate transactions. Brave is a privacy-first browser that features built-in ad blockers among other privacy-oriented features. It provides a means of accounting among advertisers, publishers, and users of a new blockchain-based digital advertising and service platform.

Name: GateToken
Description: GateToken (GT) is the official exchange token of the Gate.io exchange and the native token of Gatechain’s public network. The network is dedicated to on-chain safety with customizable time-delay recovery features. These allow for the recovery of blockchain assets even after the compromisation of private keys.

Name: BitDAO
Description: BitDAO is one of the largest Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAO) in the world. BIT is the governance token for the BitDAO ecosystem, which gives users the ability to propose and vote. The project's goal is to create a decentralized tokenized economy that is accessible to everyone. By developing BitDAO DeFi products and supporting DeFi partners and projects, BitDAO will allocate funds for the growth of the DeFi ecosystem.

Name: Loopring
Description: Loopring (LRC) is a smart contract protocol built on the Ethereum blockchain. It is designed for building decentralized crypto exchanges. This is accomplished through an automated execution system that trades across various crypto-token exchanges, protecting its users from counterparty risk and reducing the trading cost.

Name: Synthethix Network Token
Description: The SNX protocol provides an on-chain access to a wide variety of both crypto and non-crypto assets. It is an Ethereum blockchain-based DeFi protocol that allows access to highly liquid financial assets (synths). Rather than holding the underlying asset directly, synths track and provide their returns. By introducing non-blockchain assets, the platform aims to broaden the cryptocurrency space and provide access to a more robust financial market.

Name: Convex Finance
Description: Convex’s goal is to simplify Curve’s staking with the help of its native fee-earning token - CVX. Liquidity providers on Curve can earn fees and boost CRV without locking their own.

Name: NEM
Description: XEM is the native cryptocurrency of NEM's NIS1 public blockchain platform, an ecosystem of platforms that use blockchain and cryptography to provide solutions to businesses and individuals. In contrast to Proof-of-Work (PoW) and Proof-of-Stake (PoS), NIS1 uses its own algorithm, proof-of-importance (PoI). In short, PoIit ensures that transaction data on the network is recorded and processed in a timely and honest manner.

Name: Curve DAO Token
Description: CRV is the native token of Curve which is a decentralized exchange for stablecoins. Curve is moreover a decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) that uses the Ethereum-based creation tool, Aragon. Through the tool, multiple smart contracts can connect to users' deposited liquidity.

Name: Oasis Network
Description: OASIS offers a scalable and privacy-centric layer-1 blockchain network with low gas fees and high throughput. By combining confidential computing with blockchain, a new paradigm called tokenized data is created. Users can earn rewards by staking their data with applications that wish to analyze it or control how sensitive information is used.

Name: Secret
Description: SCRT is the native coin of the Secret Network, which is used to pay fees and transfers. It is a privacy-centered blockchain built on the Cosmos (ATOM) ecosystem, featuring smart contracts known as Secret Contracts. These contracts allow the transmission of encrypted inputs into encrypted outputs without the actual exposing data.

Name: Link
Description: Link (LN) is a new form of investment asset with scarcity and a project launched by LINE with the goal to create a token economy and blockchain platform for mainstream users. LN is distributed to contributors in the LINE token economy who use and expand LINE’s services.

Name: Mina Protocol
Description: Mina is acknowledged as the world's lightest blockchain because of its ability to maintain a constant size despite growth in usage. Using this protocol, DApps can run more efficiently due to its minimal "succinct blockchain" built to reduce computational requirements. In comparison to Bitcoin's 300 GB blockchain, the entire Mina network takes up only 22 KB.

Name: Decred
Description: DCR is a blockchain-based cryptocurrency established in 2016. The protocol and currency place a strong emphasis on sustainability, transparent governance, and community input. The foundations of Decred are heavily influenced by Bitcoin and follow many of its core principles.

Name: Bitkub Coin
Description: KUB is the native utility token of the Bitkub Chain, which powers, operates, and governs its ecosystem. In addition to transferring KUB from one to another, it also enables you to pay transaction fees, and redeem trading fee credits through the Bitkub Exchange. Currently, Bitkub Chain has over 4 million transactions and more than 1.4 millíon wallets registered.

Name: Holo
Description: Holochain is used to create decentralized applications to be hosted on Holo, a peer-to-peer distributed platform. This is a framework for developing DApps without the need for blockchain technology. Through Holo, Holochain-based apps can be developed and made accessible to a broader community. DApps are easily accessible through the Holo ecosystem as they are hosted on the internet by Holo network members.

Name: Compound
Description: COMP is an ERC-20 governance token of the Compound Protocol. Changes to the protocol are discussed, proposed, and voted on by COMP token holders and their delegates.

Name: yearn.finance
Description: Yearn.finance is an Ethereum-based token that governs the decentralized finance (DeFi) yearn.finance platform. It simplifies DeFi for investors who are not technically minded and lets them optimize their earnings through lending of crypto assets.

Name: SafeMoon
Description: SafeMoon is community-driven decentralized finance (DeFi) token on the Binance Smar Chain (BSC). The protocol features three main functions; reflection, liquidity pool acquisition, and manual burn with all of them taking place during each trade. In each transaction, the token charges 10% of the transaction fee, of which 5% goes to token holders and 5% goes to the Binance Coin (BNB), which is controlled by the coin's authors.

Name: XDC Network
Description:
# XDC Crypto Analysis of the Xinfin Network

![XinFin XDC Network launched in 2017](https://images.surferseo.art/05a1d8d5-c18c-43ce-9e17-c1a24bc5b478.jpeg)

Launching out of Singapore in 2017 the Xinfin Network is a highly secure and scalable next-generation blockchain. The project was created by the non-profit eXchange inFinate (XinFin) and aimed specifically at trade finance and payments. The XinFin Network is the world's first hybrid Layer 1 blockchain, meaning it can process both public and private blockchains simultaneously. This synthesis of permissioned and permissionless blockchains is advantageous to trade finance as it can be customized to industry needs where both privacy and public accountability are required.

## What is the Xinfin Network Consensus Mechanism?

The Xinfin Network started as a fork of JP Morgan’s Quorum (which was itself based on a fork of [Ethereum](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ethereum)).

The consensus mechanism (that allows users of the network to reach an agreement on transactions or the state of the network) is Xinfin's Delegated Proof-of-Stake (XDPoS). Similar to Proof of Stake (PoS), which incentivizes users to confirm data and maintain security via staking collateral, XDPoS also uses real-time voting to democratically enable a more decentralised and secure ledger. Transaction signing and verification are performed by a web of Know Your Customer (KYC) masternodes, which makes the blockchain regulatory compliant and enterprise ready.

The XDPoS allows the XDC Network to run up to 2000 transactions per second (with solutions available to expand this to 20,000) and can perform settlements near instantaneously.

The hybrid XDPoS has several advantages over existing public or private blockchains. For example, flexible settings; the ledger can be customised according to the needs of the user. Anonymous network members; KYC solves the issue of anonymous member usage, which is considered a hurdle to more enterprise adoption. In addition, near-zero transaction fees allows the Xinfin Network to offer microtransactions and facilitate much higher transaction volume.

## What is the XDC Coin?

The XDC token is the underlying utility coin that powers the XDC hybrid blockchain. XDC coins are needed to pay transaction fees as well as act as settlement mechanisms for Decentralised Applications (DApps) running on the XDC Network. For users running a KYC masternode, XDC can be staked for XDC’s block production rewards. The current price of the XDC token is {{currentprice}} and it has a circulating supply of {{circulatingsupply}} out of a max supply of 37,782,047,825. The current market cap sits at {{marketcap}}.

## Who is the Team Behind the XDC Network?

Atul Khekade, Ritesh Kakka, and Karan Bhardwaj are the three Singapore-based Co-Founders and all have long and extensive histories in the blockchain space. Khekade demonstrated the first private blockchain system for a consortium of leading Asian banks. He has founded multiple multi-million dollar companies. He works closely with banking partners, governments and institutional investors.

Kakka has extensive experience in Cloud Hosting, Cloud Setup, and Internet Infrastructure. Andre Casterman joined the core team in an advisory capacity. Casterman has over 30 years of experience in the finance sector, with 25 of those at SWIFT. He specializes in implementing and commercialising trade finance, advanced payments, and capital market innovation. Much of his work has focused on forming partnerships between fintech and institutional and commercial entities.

![ex-SWIFT Andre Casterman joins XDC Network](https://images.surferseo.art/a4207c9e-2d5b-4018-82d8-1ed704fd462c.jpeg)

## What Partners are the Xinfin Network Working With?

The XDC Network Ecosystem is committed to the growth of the Xinfin Network. In trade finance, the XDC Network is partnered with the finance distribution platform TradeTeq. TradeTeq opens trade finance to global capital markets and empowers banks to distribute trade finance assets. Their website boasts $1.5billion in volume processed, with over 5000 corporates funded. In mid-2022 TradeTeq chose the XDC hybrid blockchain to host its TRADA token, which aims to link SMEs to capital sources.

XDC Network is compatible with the ISO20022 messaging system, which means the network can interoperate with other banking systems, ERP systems, and the SWIFT interbank settlement system. Thanks to this messaging compatibility, the Xinfin Network has partnered with cost-efficient SWIFT competitor Impel to make the XDC token available as collateral for settlement.

R3 and the XDC Network have formed a partnership, that allows XDC to be a settlement token for Corda, R3's permissioned, institution-facing blockchain platform. The peer-to-peer DLT architecture firm DASL will facilitate a bridge between Corda and XDC Network. This will allow interoperability with other DLTs and legacy systems.

![XDC Network - R3 Corda bridge will allow interoperability ](https://images.surferseo.art/95075ee6-6771-4b04-af2a-505644f58214.png)

## RoadMap 2022 

Looking ahead to the rest of 2022, the XDC Foundation lays out several points of focus including:

-   The introduction of a state-of-the-art NFT marketplace on the network aimed at global brands and content creators.
    
-   The development and implementation of a transparently-sourced stablecoin on the chain with TradeTeq.
    
-   More Institutional custody solutions, exchange listings, and on-ramps for XDC.
    
-   Upgrades to the XDCPay wallet and more integrations with industry-leading institutional and retail wallets.
    
-   More staking opportunities for XDC community members.
    

## XDC Competitors

For smart contract functionality, XDC is in competition with other well-established chains in the space such as [Ethereum](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ethereum), Binance Smart Chain, [Avalanche](https://coinrotator.app/coin/avalanche-2) and [Solana](https://coinrotator.app/coin/solana).

For enterprise-facing or payment settlements, XDC can see competition from [XRP](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ripple), [Algorand](https://coinrotator.app/coin/algorand) or [Hedera](https://coinrotator.app/coin/hedera-hashgraph) Hashgraph.

Competition from private blockchains comes from Corda and Hyperledger Fabric, although both of these chains don't have their own native token and as stated, Corda can use XDC for settlement.

## Is the XDC Coin a Good Investment and What is a Possible Price Prediction?

Are we seeing a severe price correction before resuming the uptrend to all-time highs, or is this the beginning of a more sustained bear market? Looking back at the historical data for XDC price action we see a steady rise in the XDC throughout 2020, to reach an all-time high price of {{ath}} in 2021; price action that was also seen throughout the cryptocurrency markets at the time.

Some technical analysis traders using a combination of tools such as moving averages or identifying divergence and momentum are calling for a further retest of recent July 2022 lows. Other veteran traders, such as Realvison's Raoul Pal, using both technical and fundamental analysis, are forecasting that the bottom may well be in and these current prices may provide an optimal entry level. Currently trading {{currentprice}} XDC is {{percentagefromath}} from it's ATH price of {{ath}} and presents a good time to start taking the project seriously again.

Traders and investors should do their own research and remember that past performance is no guarantee of future price performance, and this is not intended as investment advice.

### TLDR

The XDC Network has provided a robust solution to the challenges faced by existing and earlier blockchain technology; namely low throughput, high feeds, and a less optimal developer experience. The ability to process both public and private networks creates much potential for greater enterprise adoption of blockchain, especially in the arena of trade finance.

Name: PAX Gold
Description: Pax Gold is a cryptocurrency backed by gold launched in 2019. PAXG is an ERC-20 token operating on the Ethereum blockchain so it is available for trade on many exchanges. Pax Gold’s premise is to make trading gold easier as the commodity itself is not easy to divide and transport.

Name: Smooth Love Potion
Description: Smooth Love Potion, or SLP, is a token in the Axie Infinity game’s economy, replacing experience points. SLP is used to breed new Axies, with cost ranging from 100 SLP to 2,100 SLP depending on how many times you breed the same Axie. SLP is an ERC-20 token and is acquired by playing the game or purchasing in the open market.

Name: Nexus Mutual
Description: Nexus Mutual is a decentralized insurance protocol which enables people to take out covers on smart contracts. The way Nexes Mutual operates is that there’s a risk-sharing pool, and people can either stake their assets in the pool for future rewards or purchase on insurance cover. NXM is the native token of the protocol.

Name: Qtum
Description: Qtum is a smart contract open-sourced blockchain and at the same time, a value transfer protocol. Qtum wants to combine the advantages of Ethereum and Bitcoin together on one chain. It is built on Bitcoin’s UTXO transaction model but it also possesses smart contracts and DApps functionality

Name: Pocket Network
Description: Operating on a PoS consensus mechanism, Pocket Network is a blockchain data platform that coordinates and distributes data at scale with a purpose to create seamless interactions between blockchains and applications. POKT is its native currency, used for staking to run validator nodes, transaction fees, and receiving network throughput.

Name: ICON
Description: ICON Network is a blockchain hub that connects different blockchains with each other via Blockchain Transmission protocol (BTP). ICON supports cross-chain token swaps and smart contract calls. A few blockchains that are integrated in ICON’s BTP are Binance Smart Chain, Near, Ethereum, Polkadot…

Name: Livepeer
Description: Livepeer was the first decentralized live video streaming network protocol, aimed to become an alternative to centralized broadcasting companies. Livepeer allows producers to submit their work on the platform and also to distribute that content to users and other streaming platforms.

Name: Bancor Network Token
Description: Launched in 2017, Bancor was the first DeFi protocol. It allows users to earn money by staking tokens with full protection from impermanent loss. Bancor offers up to 60% APR on a variety of tokens (ETH, LINK, MATIC, AAVE…). BNT, or Bancor Network Token, is the protocol token used to provide liquidity for the pools available on the network.

Name: Sushi
Description: SushiSwap (SUSHI) is an automated market maker (AMM). A decentralized exchange that provides markets for any given pair of tokens. SushiSwap's main objective is to diversify the AMM market and also provide its users with features not present in past AMMs, such as Uniswap.

Name: 1inch
Description: 1INCH is the native token of the decentralized exchange 1Inch. The Ethereum-based token is used to vote on protocol parameters under the decentralized autonomous organization (DAO) model. Through the platform, users can discover and create the most efficient ways to swap across decentralized exchanges, bringing the best prices to the market.

Name: Ankr
Description: Ankr is a decentralized blockchain infrastructure provider that has more than 50 Proof-of-Stake networks operating around the world. The network's infrastructure drives cryptocurrency-economy growth while powering a full set of multi-chain tools for Web3 users.

Name: Render Token
Description: RenderToken (RNDR) deploys a distributed GPU rendering network that is built on the Ethereum blockchain. It connects artists and studios with mining partners who are willing to share their GPU computing power. These node operators are in return rewarded with the RNDR token which can be bought and sold on exchanges.

Name: Zilliqa
Description: Zilliqa is an open-source, permission-free blockchain that can handle thousands of transactions per second. As a second-layer scaling solution, it intends to address the problem of blockchain scalability and speed. The platform hosts many decentralized applications on the platform, as well as staking and yield farming.

Name: Gnosis
Description: GNO is the native Ethereum-based token of the Gnosis Chain. The network builds decentralized infrastructure for the Ethereum ecosystem. Its primary goal is to become a decentralized prediction market that uses the crowd to make predictions.

Name: Marinade Staked SOL
Description: Marinade.finance is the very first liquid staking protocol built on the Solana blockchain. The protocol allows users to stake and lock their SOL and in return receive MSOL, which can be freely used in DeFi. The project is moreover supported by the Solana Foundation themselves.

Name: Ravencoin
Description: Ravencoin is a digital peer-to-peer (P2P) network and is a blockchain-based service providing use case-specific functionality. The system was built to handle the specific function of transferring assets from one party to another efficiently by determining the truth about who owns what asset.

Name: Kava
Description: KAVA is the governance token of the cross-chain DeFi platform Kava. It's stablecoin lending system (powered by USDX), built on the Cosmos blockchain, utilizes collateralized debt positions (CDPs) to guarantee loans are always adequately back stopped. Borrowers who fail to maintain collateral above a threshold will have their assets seized by the Kava liquidator and sold through the auction module.

Name: IOST
Description: The IOST network is an ultra-fast, decentralized and fully functioning ecosystem with its own nodes, wallets, and a new consensus protocol based on the concept of proof-of-believability. The high number of transactions per second, the scalability and security of the blockchain, and the privacy protection it offers provide online service providers with infinite possibilities to serve their customers.

Name: WOO Network
Description: Woo Network provides traders, exchanges, institutions, and DeFi platforms with easy, low-cost access to best-in-class liquidity and trading execution. Holding WOO tokens enables users in both CeFi and DeFi products in the network to reduce fees and staking to very low costs.

Name: Moonbeam
Description: The Moonbeam blockchain is an Ethereum-compatible smart contract platform on Polkadot. Moonbeam provides an easy way for Ethereum developers to build or deploy Solidity projects within a Substrate environment.

Name: LooksRare
Description: Looks is the native token of the LooksRare community-run NFT marketplace.There you’ll be able to buy or sell NFTs and even receive rewards for participating.

Name: 0x
Description: 0x (ZRX) is a protocol that enables token exchanges through Ethereum's blockchain by providing both smart contract infrastructure and liquidity. Holders can use ZRX to propose changes to the protocol and participate in platform governance.

Name: Radio Caca
Description: Radio Caca is a decentralized organization run by internet-savvy people from all around the world. They are committed to building a brave and innovative virtual world. In addition, Radio Caca is the exclusive manager of the mystery box NFT Maye Musk.

Name: e-Radix
Description: The Radix protocol is a layer-1 distributed ledger designed to support decentralized finance applications using a highly scalable and composable consensus design called 'Cerberus'.nsus design. eXRD is an ERC-20 token that will be swappable for XRD, the platform utility token of Radix, when the Radix mainnet opens to the public.

Name: Immutable X
Description: The Immutable X protocol is Ethereum's first zero-knowledge (zk-rollup) scaling solution for gas-free NFT mining and trading, developed in partnership with StarkWare. They enable trading of NFTs in an open, decentralized environment, secured by Ethereum.

Name: Horizen
Description: Horizen is a privacy-focused cryptocurrency that combines cutting-edge technology with cutting-edge privacy features. They aim to bring thousands of real-life services to the world using their sidechains, which will allow anyone to create privacy-based applications.

Name: Velas
Description: The Velas blockchain takes advantage of artificial intelligence-enhanced DPOS (AIDPOS) consensus for high volume transaction processing, which does not compromise decentralization and security.

Name: Ontology
Description: Ontology is a decentralized identity and data solution that brings trust, privacy, and security to Web3. With its regulatory-compliant digital identity solutions, individuals and enterprises can rest assured that their privacy is protected through access to Web3.

Name: Rocket Pool
Description: Rocket Pool is a staking pool for Ethereum 2.0. The protocol aims to lower both capital and hardware requirements for staking on Ethereum 2.0, therefore making Ethereum more decentralized and secure. In order to accomplish this, Rocket Pool provides users with trustless stakes against a network of node operators.

Name: Sapphire
Description: Sapphire coin (SAPP coin) is a stable method of payment that will be used in the real economy and will be given only to reliable partners who add value to the development project. Its goal is to make sure the coin is easy to use, ensures fast payment transfers, and most importantly, lives and breathes in its community.

Name: dYdX
