Description: With Nash, users can send assets securely without having to rely on intermediaries, enabling decentralized finance to become fast, simple, and secure. The speed and functionality of traditional services are combined with the security benefits of blockchain. Thanks to the matching engine, the decentralized exchange is extremely fast.

Name: sAUD
Description: sAUD is a synthetix-based asset that tracks the price of AUD through the price feeds supplied by a chainlink oracle.sAUD is a synthetix-based asset that tracks the price of AUD through the price feeds supplied by a chainlink oracle.sAUD is a synthetix-based asset that tracks the price of AUD through the price feeds supplied by a chainlink oracle.

Name: NuriFootBall
Description: The Nuri Football platform is a blockchain-based football platform made by Nuri Football Team. Through the platform, amateur players and teams will be able to share videos and receive rewards, as well as manage statistics for their performances and teams. In addition to offering match result predictions, they also plan to feature various NFTs and even metaverses.

Name: Dope Wars Paper
Description: The PAPER token is a token used within the DOPE WARS game/ecosystem. DOPE WARS is an Ethereum and StarkWare blockchain game that is based on the classic TI-83 games. Additionally, they are continuing NFT works as well as decentralized governance experiments using a DAO.

Name: Gaia Everworld
Description: With Gaia Everworld, the player creates their own lands, researches the lands, collects, breeds, and battles their Gaia Legionnaires in an immersive, multi-region fantasy world. The Gaia Everworld cryptocurrency is part of the new generation of games built on the blockchain known as play-to-earn (P2E), allowing players to control their own characters and earn rewards through gameplay.

Name: Cypherium
Description: With Cypherium, enterprises and institutions gain financial inclusion while delivering operational efficiencies. The Cypherium blockchain combines a hybrid consensus mechanism with decentralization and DLT characteristics to optimize commercial viability. With Cypherium, security and decentralization are maintained without sacrificing scalability.

Name: Kintsugi
Description: Kintsugi represents Interlay's first test net, a real-world experiment deployed on Kusama and with real economic value. It aims to connect crypto-currencies like Bitcoin to DeFi platforms like Polkadot and Ethereum through a decentralized network. This network will be implemented as a Polkadot parachain and be connected to Cosmos, Ethereum, and other major DeFi networks.

Name: Fancy Games
Description: Fancy Games is a blockchain game studio focusing on play-to-earn mini-games. The first title from the studio is called Fancy Birds, which is a similar version of Flappy Birds, but players can also breed new unique birds. FNC will act as both the governance and play-to-earn token for Fancy Games.

Name: Lithium Finance
Description: Lithium Finance is a protocol designed to estimate pricing for liquid assets on demand. The protocol acts as a voting machine that determines the current perceived market price of an asset without an active market by using collective intelligence.

Name: NewYorkCityCoin
Description: NewYorkCityCoin is part of the CityCoins project, which lets NYC holders earn STX and BTC, while allocating funds to treasuries reserved for the city of New York.

Name: Uniform Fiscal Object
Description: Uniform Fiscal Object, or UFO, is a cryptocurrency that describes itself as a younger brother of Bitcoin, As a result, it inherits characteristics like decentralization and PoW consensus mechanism. UFO can be used for p2p payments, public payments, intellectual property registration and in decentralized exchanges. UFO blends multiple hashing algorithms to enable strong encryption, thus better safety.

Name: Bankless DAO
Description: Bankless DAO is a decentralized autonomous organization with a mission to support the bankless movement towards a world where people are free from the traditional financial system. Bankless DAO aims to facilitate user-friendly onramps for people to discover decentralized financial technologies through education, media, and culture.

Name: Paint Swap
Description: PaintSwap is a decentralized exchange on Fantom, offering low trading fees, competitive APY, and a user-friendly experience. PaintSwap also provides an NFT marketplace.

Name: Eco Value Coin
Description: Eco Value Coin aims to be a ‘Carbon Credit Token’, which individuals, businesses and governments can trade to offset carbon emissions. Its goal is to streamline transactions with minimal fees and fast transfer speed.

Name: BitSong
Description: BitSong describes itself as a distributed blockchain music ecosystem that will decentralize the music sector, offering artists a transparent and fast earning model by doing away with all the intermediaries, In addition, it hopes to enable listeners to consume music and earn money in a new way.

Name: CREDITS
Description: Credits (CS) is an open-source blockchain platform with an emphasis on developing dApps and smart contracts with improved security, decentralization, and scalability. The Credits team reports to have achieved up to 1 million transactions per second, a 0.1s confirmation time and fees as low as 0.001 USD.

Name: THORWallet DEX
Description: THORWallet DEX offers a non-custodial wallet with many features such as multi-chain native token swapping, synthetic asset trading and the ability to invest in multi-chain saving accounts.

Name: MUSK Gold
Description: MUSK Gold is a decentralized, p2p asset that allows users to send and receive money anywhere in an easy manner. It also acts as a reward token for everyday utilities in sectors like sustainable energy, electric vehicles, and advanced communication.

Name: Ledgis
Description: LEDGIS is a blockchain service that prevents IT platforms from monopolizing user data. Users that contribute to the ecosystem can receive rewards accordingly and also experience many dApp services at the speed of 3,000 transactions per second.

Name: Euphoria
Description: Euphoria is an algorithmic reserve currency protocol on Harmony. Its native token is WAGMI, which is backed by a cluster of assets in the Euphoria Treasury. As a result, WAGMI cannot fall below a certain value. Besides, Euphoria also uses staking and purchasing bonds to integrate economic and game-theoretic dynamics into the market.

Name: EnterDAO
Description: EnterDAO has a mission to build products to create new markets in the metaverse economy such as renting/lending metaverse land, or entering various metaverses via only one application. Holders of the ENTR token are granted voting rights in the governance of EnterDAO.

Name: Shibaverse
Description: ShibaVerse is a metaverse bringing to users virtual reality, digital real estate, NFTs, play-to-earn…all in one environment. People can use the platform’s native currency VERSE for purchasing real estates and other NFT assets, as well as utilizing play-to-earn features.

Name: TONToken
Description: The Telegram Open Network (TON) claims to be a fast, secure, scalable blockchain that is able to host all reasonable applications that can be currently conceived. TON aims to be a huge distributed supercomputer that is both user-friendly and service provider-friendly.

Name: Unicly
Description: Unicly aims to be the best NFT gallery and trading platform by combining AMMs, NFT auctions, farming, and decentralized governance. Uniquely caters to every kind of users, be it collectors, casual investors, artists/creators, or yield farmers. UNIC is the native token of the platform, which has a maximum supply of 1 million and allows holders to participate in governance votings.

Name: Biconomy Exchange Token
Description: Biconomy Exchange Token, or BIT, is a decentralized digital asset built on the Binance Smart Chain and it is BEP20 compliant. BIT’s value extends beyond the reputation of the Biconomy Exchange; it gives the community of the exchange more involvement in using it, and the chance to receive rewards for their involvement.

Name: MetaVPad
Description: MetaVPad is a launchpad that aims to support promising projects that are metaverse-related. It hopes to position itself as a hub of platforms, protocols, tools, and services in the metaverse.

Name: Meme
Description: MEME is the native token of the platform DontBuyMEME, which hosts digital artists worldwide and lets its community mint NFTs by staking MEME tokens. The MEME token can also be utilized to bid on art auctions on the platform.

Name: Sinverse
Description: Sinverse is a metaverse with a focus on social play-to-earn games and gambling applications. The game will take players to the most scandalous cities around the world with digital real estate available for purchase. Players can develop their own empire on these lands, such as building clubs, venues for online events or competing in underworld activities.

Name: CUTcoin
Description: CUTcoin is a pure PoS coin that offers anonymous transactions and its own privacy token: CNT. In fact, every token built on the CUTcoin chain benefits from the same privacy features as the native CUT tokens and Monero. The deployment of CNT is also made in a way that anyone without coding knowledge can still do it with one single command.

Name: sLINK
Description: sLINK is a synthetic Chainlink token designed to track Chainlink’s price using data supplied by Chainlink’s network of oracles.

Name: Game Fantasy Token
Description: Game Fantasy Token (GFT) serves as currency to the economy of StarCrazy, a blockchain game empowered by NFTs. The StarCrazy team claims to prioritize the entertainment aspect unlike many other blockchain games.

Name: Zodium
Description: Zodium is a play-to-earn game that features 3D NFT characters inspired by the ancient oriental legends of the 12 zodiac animals and star signs. ZODI is both Zodium’s utility and governance token that lets users play games, vote on important decisions, and stake to claim rewards.

Name: veDAO
Description: veDAO is a decentralized autonomous organization with the purpose of giving ordinary people the chance to own and control a portion of Solidly, an exchange for swapping supported stable and volatile assets.

Name: Hundred Finance
Description: Hundred Finance is a dApp used for lending and borrowing cryptocurrencies. It maintains market health and stability by integrating with Chainlink oracles, and it specializes in serving long-tail assets.

Name: Annex Finance
Description: Annex Finance is a platform for lending and borrowing cryptocurrencies. It offers a DEX swap for users to create LP (Liquidity Provider) tokens with high APY returns. In addition, the platform has an NFT marketplace as well as an IDO auction.

Name: AstroSwap
Description: AstroSwap is a decentralized exchange built on the Velas network, claiming to be the fastest DEX for the Cardano ecosystem. Users can stake tokens in the ‘Galaxy Pools’ to earn rewards, swap Cardano tokens with rapid speed and low fees, and play the AstroSwap Space Lottery to win prizes.

Name: Creampye [OLD]
Description: Creampye is building the PYECharts, PYESwap, and PYEWallet to revolutionize the future of cryptocurrency, Their mission is to offer better decentralized tech and more affordable access to everyone.

Name: Mars Ecosystem Token
Description: Mars Ecosystem is the new decentralized stablecoin model, which integrates the creation and the use of stablecoin into one ecosystem.The Mars Ecosystem Token, XMS, is used to support the liquidity of Mars stablecoins. It can also be utilized for governance rights and earning parts of the transaction fees through staking.

Name: ArchAngel
Description: ArchAngel is a community-driven project with a goal to accelerate the adoption of cryptocurrencies. The ArchAngel token (ARCHA) uses the innovative Distribution of Value (DoV) ecosystem model to let projects within the ecosystem integrate their solutions across a common framework to support each other’s growth.

Name: LaunchZone
Description: LaunchZone is a platform built around the Binance Smart Chain (BSC) network. It offers non-custodial cryptocurrency exchanges. It's a service that allows Binance's off-chain functionality to be available on the BSC network.

Name: Blizz Finance
Description: Blizz Finance allows people to lend or borrow money using a decentralized, non-custodial liquidity market protocol based on the Avalanche blockchain. Those in need can apply for funding on its website, while contributors may earn passive income.

Name: Cap
Description: CAP is a blockchain protocol that enables decentralized trading. With CAP, traders can trade synthetic assets using leveraged trading without fear of censorship. With CAP, traders can trade synthetic assets by using leverage. Users select their asset and leverage, then make predictions on whether the asset's value will rise or fall by executing a position. Losses are added to a treasury and winnings are subtracted from it. It serves as CAP's war chest for increased growth, advertising, development, audit, profit, or saving.

Name: POA Network
Description: POA Network's smart contract framework is open-source, Ethereum-based, and built on the POA blockchain. It consists of a sidechain built on Ethereum utilizing Proof of Autonomy as its consensus mechanism. It uses a pre-selected group of validators to secure the network. These validators are publicly identified. This protocol provides flexibility to code in Ethereum standards while providing scalability and interoperability in blockchain networks.

Name: Sport and Leisure
Description: SnL tokens are Ethereum-backed assets that eliminate any counterparty, market, or security risks. The SnL smart contract enables trust-less transactions using blockchain and smart contract technologies, allowing bet makers to set their own odds, be paid out when they win, and resolve all disputes through a patented marketplace voting system.

Name: Hubble
Description: HBB is the Hubble Protocol's native token. A decentralized protocol built on Solana, Hubble Protocol utilizes blockchain technology. The protocol's native token can also be held and staked by users, making USDH worth 90.9% LTV, yielding positive interest for collateral deposits (earn while borrowed), providing liquidity via a Stability Pool, and maintaining the health of the system by participating in our in-house liquidations. Users can also deposit multiple assets, mint USDH, and earn positive interest on collateral deposits.

Name: SmartCredit Token
Description: SmartCredit.io is a Decentral Finance application for crypto borrowing and lending. One of the crucial differences between SmartCredit.io and most other DeFi lending solutions today is that it focuses on fixed-income funds rather than money market funds. A SMARTCREDIT token can be used as a utility token or loyalty token on the SmartCredit.io platform. The token can also be used to collect liquidity to support borrowers, lenders, and extensions.

Name: X
Description: X is a network of decentralized NFT marketplaces, owned and operated by the X community. With X tokens staked for the veX governance token, users obtain voting rights through the X DAO. It is a community with an emphasis on radical self-expression and inclusiveness, and there are no prerequisites for membership.

Name: BNS Token
Description: BNS is a multi-protocol utility token by Bitbns, designed to allow general users to transact with cryptocurrency just as they would with FIAT currency in day-to-day transactions. As well as being a fundamentally useful form of money, BNS could be used in complex operations such as Banking, Loan Disbursements, Cryptocurrency Investments, and more.

Name: Solidex
Description: With Solidex, Solidly users can optimize yield, voting power, and liquidity provisioning. Token holders deposit their LP tokens into Solidex for the chance to earn SOLID rewards with boosted rewards without having to lock SOLID themselves. Lockers that support Solid receive a portion of the protocol revenue in exchange for boosting Solid's revenue.

Name: Sentinel [OLD]
Description: This is the old Sentinel (DVPN) token that has since transitioned to the Cosmos ecosystem. Sentinel is a network of decentralized VPNs that aims to reduce the carbon footprint by using shared resources.

Name: Viper
Description: Founded on the Harmony Blockchain, Viper Protocol is a cross-chain and multiproduct protocol. It is part of VenomDAO, a DAO operating on several new and emerging blockchains compatible with EVM- and Ethereum-tools, such as Harmony, Binance Smart Chain, Fantom.

Name: CRYPTO20
Description: CRYPTO20, launched in December 2017, is the world's first crypto index fund tokenized with digital tokens. Fund holdings are rebalanced weekly, and each token represents 10% of the fund's value, allowing for weekly tracking of the 20 top crypto assets by market cap.

Name: Luna Rush
Description: The Luna Rush (LUS) is a blockchain-based idle role-playing game (RPG) that leverages the Binance Smart Chain (BSC). Luna Rush integrates anime and strategy elements to feature warriors and heroes fighting with flamboyant effects to level up and become stronger. Depending on their preferences, players can either fight other players, cooperate with friends, or participate in tournaments. The game offers players 11 different warriors to choose from, each with their own unique skills. Warriors can be bought from the game store or summoned through the marketplace.

Name: SquidDao
Description: Based on the SQUID token, SQUID DAO offers a decentralized reserve currency protocol. Its design was inspired by OlympusDAO. Every SQUID has a treasury backed with a basket of assets (e.g. ETH), which ensures the token is valued intrinsically and shouldn't fall below that level. In the same way as OlympusDAO, SQUID DAO will leverage bonding and staking dynamics.

Name: Depo
Description: Depo is a decentralized multimarket aggregator for institutional investors engaged in crypto-token investment. Unlike other aggregators, DePo focuses on the entire crypto ecosystem, not just one aspect. It offers trading, storing, and managing your entire portfolio of crypto assets.

Name: Aleph Zero
Description:
# A Complete Aleph Zero Analysis

![Aleph Zero Privacy Enhancing Blockchain Technology for Mass Adoption](https://images.surferseo.art/25d7ee85-349a-4e16-8ba9-345a824bc820.png)

## How The Aleph Zero Network Started

Aleph Zero is a layer 1 public blockchain that uses novel privacy-enhancing technology, and a Directed Acyclic Graph ([DAG](https://coinrotator.app/coin/constellation-labs))-based consensus protocol to solve some of the shortcomings inherent in existing alternatives. The open nature of public blockchains, whereby balances and transactions are visible as a public record, is seen as undesirable by many participants who wish to maintain anonymity in their dealings; for example, governments, institutions, or corporations. In addition, while many Small Medium size Businesses (SMBs) see the benefits of the security and decentralization offered by blockchain solutions, the scalability, and the ability to process information at scale, make existing solutions somewhat impractical. Aleph Zero uses a DAG-based solution to this, promising high processing speeds while maintaining decentralization and security, in other words solving the Blockchain trilemma. The project was conceived in 2018, with the mainnet going live in 2021.

## The AlephBFT Consensus protocol

The code that underwrites Aleph Zeros is the peer-reviewed consensus protocol AlephBFT. AlephBFT combines a modified variation of Proof-of-Stake with Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs). To help break down what this all means let us start with the basics.

The consensus protocol is the engine that powers every blockchain. It allows the ledgers to agree (form consensus) on a single version of ‘truth’ (the state of the network, a balance, or a transaction). The two most dominant protocols are Proof-of-Work (as used by [Bitcoin](https://coinrotator.app/coin/bitcoin)) and Proof-of-Stake (as currently used by [Ethereum](https://coinrotator.app/coin/ethereum) 2.0). PoS is recognized as being more secure, less resource intensive, and more scalable than PoW.

The AlephBFT uses a modified version of PoS that uses a rotating, as opposed to a fixed, committee of validators. Fixed committees are a pool of validators that decide on things like block production and the direction of the network. In the AlephBFT protocol a new committee of validators is chosen every 15mins, so 96 unique committees in each 24hr period. Having a rotating committee enables a higher degree of security and decentralization.

### Merging PoS with DAG

The aforementioned modified PoS is merged with distributed ledger technology DAG to increase throughput (the amount of information that can be processed), transaction speed, and low transaction cost. DAGs are mathematically complex, but in a very simplified form, they allow for data or transactions to be processed in parallel, as opposed to a linear fashion, one by one, as in a [conventional blockchain](https://coinrotator.app/?category=Blockchain). Of note here is that while AlephBFT uses DAG it remains a blockchain. This is because the DAG acts as an auxiliary structure to collect information on the order of transactions. The blockchain is constructed secondarily from these data.

### Byzantine Fault Tolerance

Finally, Byzantine Fault Tolerance architecture is applied to the protocol. This ensures that even if up to ⅓ of the network becomes compromised by bad faith actors, the blockchain can still operate security and produce an immutable and proven transaction history.

### Aleph Zero Framework Built with Substrate Stack

The privacy framework will be enabled by technology known as ZK-SNARKS, and secure Multi-Party Computation (sMPC). ZK-SNARKS allow network users to prove they possess certain data without revealing the exact content of that data. For example, making a purchase without revealing their wallet balance. SMPC’s can keep sensitive data off-chain for certain nodes, only to be communicated when relevant nodes perform a secure ‘handshake’. This framework is built using the open source Substrate, allowing it to interoperate with chains such as Polkadot while being flexible, modular, and adaptable to a wide range of modifications.

### Use Cases for the Aleph Zero Platform

With the privacy and anonymity that comes with ZK SNARKS and SMPC, coupled with the vast throughput, speed and low transaction cost enabled by DAG technology, Aleph Zero claims to have identified many key areas that they argue would benefit from the decentralized architecture offered by their protocol.

-   [Internet of things](https://coinrotator.app/?category=IOT)
    
-   Private smart Contracts
    
-   Supply Chain Management
    
-   Asset Digitalisation
    
-   [Virtual Game Assets](https://coinrotator.app/?category=Gaming)
    
-   Decentralized Domain Name System
    
-   [Universal Wallet](https://coinrotator.app/?category=Web+3.0) and Decentralised Exchange
    
-   Notary Signatory
    
-   Transparent Public Document Access
    
-   Automatic Tax Payments
    
-   Databases
    
-   Automatic Payments and Shared Revenue Agreements
    

## The Aleph Zero Team

The world-class team is comprised of over 30 members from a diverse set of disciplines with impressive scientific and business backgrounds, and achievements under their belts such as ACM ICPC World Finals, first prize in the International Mathematics Competition, and a Simons-Berkeley Research Fellowship. The Co-founders are listed as Matthew Niemerg, Ph.D. Adam Gagol, Ph.D., Antoni Zolciak, Michal Swietek, Ph.d. Project Research and Development for the project is carried out by Poland-based Cardinal Cryptography.

## The Aleph Zero Ecosystem

The Aleph Zero ecosystem boasts over 40 working collaborations on its website. 17 of these are from investment funds including names such as Genblock Capital, Bellweather Rocks, and AW3 Labs. The remaining participants come from a diverse array of sectors including legal advisory, identity management, lending and borrowing, [NFT](https://coinrotator.app/?category=NFT)s, and the metaverse.

![Aleph Zero Ecocsystem; credit Twitter user AlephZeroNews](https://images.surferseo.art/e2872bad-9d24-4395-9cce-1406b6824638.jpeg)

## The $AZERO Token

The $AZERO is the native token on the Aleph Zero blockchain. It has several utility functions, including reduced swap fees on the Decentralised Exchange, discounts on fees for wrapping assets, and the use of the coming Liminal [privacy layer](https://coinrotator.app/?category=Privacy+Coin). $AZERO will also be used for staking.

## Earning Potential via Staking

### Validators

Earning potential is made available in the form of staking as a validator. To become a validator, the minimum stake is 25,000 AZERO tokens, while the APY is currently 13%. Validators are responsible for ensuring the security and veracity of transactions on the network. All validators need to undergo a know-your-customer (KYC)verification process and there is a minimum hardware requirement.

### Nominators

Nominators can vouch for validators by delegating their staked tokens. The minimum stake requirement is 100 also with an APY of 13%. There is no hardware requirement.

## Aleph Zero Price Prediction

Looking at the price chart for $AZERO we can see it reached an all-time high of {{ath}}, with a current price at {{currentprice}} which marks a {{percentagefromath}} drawdown, which has been seen throughout the larger crypto market. The marketcap is {{marketcap}} with a circulating supply of {{circulatingsupply}} which represents {{percentagecirculatingsupply}}. For comparison, a smart contract layer 1 such as [Solana](https://coinrotator.app/?portfolio=sol), with 1.5x the supply reached an all-time high of $259, which gives an indication of what price action is possible if the chain was to gain traction. Although this is strictly not investment advice and is more to highlight a potential outcome.

## Future Releases from The Aleph Zero Foundation

The next big thing from Aleph is the launch of their interoperable hybrid privacy infrastructure layer Liminal. This multichain layer will provide confidentiality of transactions, including smart contract functionality, and a portal to other chains from Aleph to, initially, Ethereum, Near, Kusama, Cosmos, and [Binance Smart Chain](https://coinrotator.app/coin/binancecoin).

Additional events that can be expected include community validators going live on the mainnet, and a Dark Metaverse to be launched in collaboration with metaverse project DRKVRS.

![Liminal Multichain Privacy layer for Web3.0, credit: alephzero.org](https://images.surferseo.art/63927327-c260-4c8f-9447-dbd8759591cb.jpeg)
